description_long: This object is a pine cone, characterized by its woody, overlapping scales arranged in a spiral pattern around a central axis. The scales are thick, rigid, and have a rough, textured surface with a mix of brown and tan hues, giving it a natural, earthy appearance. The cone is ovoid in shape, tapering slightly towards the top, with the scales opening outward and creating a layered, three-dimensional structure. The tips of the scales are lighter in color, contrasting with the darker, inner portions. The overall form is irregular, with some scales protruding more than others, and the central stem is visible at the top. This type of tree debris is commonly found on the ground in urban parks, sidewalks, or near coniferous trees.
description: A brown, woody pine cone with thick, overlapping scales and a rough, textured surface. The scales are arranged in a spiral, creating a layered, natural appearance.
description_view_0: The pine cone is seen from a slightly angled side, showing the layered, outward-flaring scales and the central stem at the top.
description_view_1: This view highlights the top of the pine cone, with the central stem most prominent and the spiral arrangement of scales clearly visible.
description_view_2: The side view emphasizes the irregular spacing and thickness of the scales, with the cone's ovoid shape apparent.
description_view_3: A lower side view reveals the underside of the scales and the base of the cone, showing the rough, woody texture.
